introSection: Giant Dip was a wooden roller coaster located at Riverside Park in Agawam, Massachusetts, USA. It was the first roller coaster built at the park, and its popularity led to the building of the Greyhound.

sections.title: Appearances
sections.content:
* Page 8 of the May 29, 1912 issue of the Springfield Daily Republican has a picture of this roller coaster, this might mean that the Giant Dip opened on May 28.
 * A reference can also be found in the July 1913 issue of the Western New England Magazine - "...merry shouts are heard from the 'giant dip roller coaster,' and there is always a long line of young people waiting for a chance to ride."

fullText:
Giant Dip was a wooden roller coaster located at Riverside Park in Agawam, Massachusetts, USA. It was the first roller coaster built at the park, and its popularity led to the building of the Greyhound.


 * Page 8 of the May 29, 1912 issue of the Springfield Daily Republican has a picture of this roller coaster, this might mean that the Giant Dip opened on May 28.
 * A reference can also be found in the July 1913 issue of the Western New England Magazine - "...merry shouts are heard from the 'giant dip roller coaster,' and there is always a long line of young people waiting for a chance to ride."
